l1.label: Leverage Levels
l1.details: Margin $1279.0B (8th consecutive record) | HF gross: GS 285%, JPM 298% (99th pctile MS) | Multistrat 645% | Debt/GDP 4.06% (ALL-TIME HIGH)

data.notes: GS: gross rose 3rd straight year to record by end-2025. Mag7 ~19% of total US net exposure. Semis net at 5yr high.

l2.label: Momentum
l2.details: Margin YoY +36.5% (>20% threshold) | Debt/GDP 4.06% ALL-TIME HIGH above 3.5% threshold | SPX YoY +15.5% — leverage growing 2.4x faster than the market

data.notes: Leverage gap (margin YoY minus SPX YoY) = 21.0pp — extreme divergence

l3.label: Financing
l3.details: NFCI -0.5236 (tightening from -0.5629 on 2/20) | HY OAS 2.97% (widening from 2.65% trough in Jan) | CCC OAS 9.15% (widened from 8.38 trough) | MOVE 74.53 (+6.43%) | SOFR 3.66 (stable) | Conditions still overall loose BUT credit spreads widening and NFCI tightening at margin

data.notes: Key shift: NFCI has tightened 3.9bps over past month (from -0.5670 to -0.5236). HY OAS has widened 32bp from Jan trough. CCC widening even more (+67bp from Jan trough). Not yet red but direction has changed — scoring 0.5 for early tightening signal.

l4.label: Crowding
l4.details: Mag 7 = 42.1% of SPX ($21T) | NVDA alone 9.5% ($4.76T) | Semis net exposure at 5yr high | AI infra 21.4% of SPX across 10 names

data.notes: GS Q1 2026 outlook confirms Mag7 ~19% of total US HF net exposure (down from 21% peak but still elevated). Semis net at 5yr high. Software net at 5yr LOW — rotation within tech, not out of tech.

l5a.label: Vol Structure
l5a.details: VIX 26.77 (+12.72% today, +79% YTD) WELL above 22 threshold | VVIX 125.68 (+8.41%) above 110 threshold | IV 21.45% vs HV 11.81% — implied nearly 2x realized | IV Rank 33.81%, IV Percentile 92% | VIX term structure flattening

data.notes: CRITICAL: VIX has surged from 16.15 on 1/26 to 26.77 today — a 66% move in ~6 weeks. VVIX above 110 signals vol-of-vol regime shift. IV/HV ratio near 2x means the market is pricing far more risk than has been realized — protective demand is extreme.

l5b.label: Options Sentiment
l5b.details: Equity P/C 1.187 (EXTREME FEAR, well above 0.85) | 25-delta risk reversal -8.01 (at -8 threshold) | GEX: SPX 6747 BELOW flip 6894 = NEGATIVE GAMMA | Put wall 7000 / Call wall 7000 — both far above spot

data.notes: SPX is 147 pts below the gamma flip point — deep in negative gamma territory. Dealers are SHORT gamma, meaning they must SELL INTO WEAKNESS and BUY INTO STRENGTH, amplifying moves in both directions. With P/C at 1.19 and risk reversal at -8.01, protection demand is extreme. The 7000 put/call wall is 253 pts above spot — no nearby support from options positioning.

l6.label: Calendar
l6.details: FOMC + Dot Plot 3/18 (12 days) | Quad Witching OPEX 3/20 (14 days) | Quarter-End Rebalancing 3/31 (25 days) | THREE HIGH-severity events clustered in 25-day window

data.notes: FOMC + Quad Witching back-to-back is the highest-risk calendar cluster of the year. In negative gamma with elevated leverage, the forced delta hedging around OPEX could amplify any directional move from the Fed's dot plot.

turbulence.cross_asset: Gold +1.21%, Treasuries bid (10Y -0.56%), USD -0.33%, RTY -2.42% — classic risk-off rotation

regime_estimate.transition_alert: S3→S4 probability rising. VIX acceleration + negative gamma + calendar cluster = elevated transition risk.

real_time_pulse.synthesis: TAPE CONFIRMING — Real-time stress is accelerating and aligning with the structural leverage read. SPX below both 50d and 200d MA, VIX surging +79% YTD, deep negative gamma, with FOMC+OPEX cluster in 12 days. Defensive positioning warranted.

divergence_alert.notes: No divergence — Layer 5 options fear (2.0/2.0) is now CONFIRMED by Layer 3 early tightening (0.5) and real-time tape stress. This is alignment, not divergence. The prior 'Options Leading' divergence from 2/26-3/2 has resolved: structural data is catching up to what options were signaling.

score_history.note: SPX below gamma flip, VIX surging
VIX 26.77 (+12.7%), VVIX 125.68, SPX 6753 below 50d/200d MA, deep negative gamma
